intent: Two friends meet on a road in the morning. The narrator hands a small key to their friend; the friend looks at it; the friend holds it in their large hand. A complete arc — encounter, exchange, acknowledgment — built from concrete actions, not pathetic fallacy. Dialogue is deliberately deferred to story 2+ when the と-quotative grammar point can be properly introduced (it is JLPT-N4, above story 1's tier).

sentences.jp: 朝、私は道で友達を見ます。
sentences.en: Morning. I see my friend on the road.

sentences.jp: 私は手に小さい鍵を持ちます。
sentences.en: I hold a small key in my hand.

sentences.jp: 私は鍵を友達に渡します。
sentences.en: I hand the key to my friend.
My friend looks at the key.

sentences.jp: 友達は鍵を大きい手に持ちます。
sentences.en: My friend holds the key in their large hand.
